title: sorbus trilocularis
intro: Sorbus trilocularis, also known as the three-horned rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sorbus trilocularis include the potential treatment of fever, colds, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to improve digestion and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Sorbus trilocularis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
